Although GNP per capita did indeed rise, its benefit came down to only a small part of the population." So the study is indicating that even though GNP per capita increased (which is a measure of economic growth), the actual development (like health, education, etc.) didn't improve much for most people. The benefits were only for a small part of the population. So the study is saying that the economic growth didn't translate into widespread development. Now, the question is phrased as: According to the study, economic growth brought _ to some backward countries. The options are about who benefited. Option A: benefit to a third of their population.
